record_identity.title: External human identity-rights declaration interface and runtime gate

summary: Proposal to establish a vendor-neutral CAM interface for machine-readable declarations governing AI use of an identifiable natural person's or professional persona's name, image, likeness, voice, movement, signature, and related protected identity attributes. The interface would preserve declaration source, subject binding, use scope, authority, lifecycle, conditions, conflicts, and minor protections across CONTINUITY records, OPERATIONS verification, SECURITY integrity, and runtime execution without treating an external registry as Identity-domain authority or universal legal permission.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: External consent registries and machine-readable rights standards are becoming operational surfaces that AI systems may query before training, generation, adaptation, emulation, publication, or commercial use. CAM presently contains strong consent, likeness, reconstruction, provenance, source-authority, minor-protection, and runtime-arbitration doctrine, but lacks one explicit cross-domain interface for consuming an external human identity-attribute rights declaration. Without that interface, systems may treat registry absence as permission, rely on stale or forged declarations, ignore conditional clearance, strip restrictions during transfer, conflate human publicity or personality rights with CAM Identity-domain ontology, or execute solely because one machine-readable record appears permissive.
evidence_confidence: corroborated governance and interface need; legal effect and ecosystem adoption remain jurisdiction-dependent and unresolved

source_records.source_title: RSL Media Human Consent Standard 1.0 Draft Specification
source_records.model_or_algorithm: machine-readable rights declaration and clearance protocol
source_records.deployment_context: Draft specification for machine-readable AI training and generation permissions, prohibitions, conditions, trusted-host or trusted-certification evaluation, declaration lifecycle, and protected identity attributes.
source_records.source_context: The draft distinguishes AI training from AI generation; defines human identity scopes including name, image, likeness, voice, movement, and signature; states that absent or non-operative declarations are not permission; requires current and trusted declaration evaluation; and makes purported permissions for identifiable persons under 18 non-operative while allowing protective prohibitions.
source_records.relevance_note: Primary external technical and governance source for a vendor-neutral CAM declaration-consumption interface.

primary_artefact_access.access_method: public web review of official draft specification
limitations: The specification is a draft and states that it must not be used for production implementation.
Legal validity and rights ownership remain jurisdiction- and fact-dependent.

source_records.interpretive_reliance: Used to identify interoperable declaration states, trust, subject binding, scope, lifecycle, conflict, minor-protection, and audit requirements without importing RSL terminology as CAM source authority.

source_records.source_title: Actor and Producer Cate Blanchett and EU Parliament Member Eva Maydell Launch RSL Media Human Consent Registry to Protect Identity in the Age of AI
source_records.deployment_context: Public launch of a registry through which people or authorised representatives may declare how AI systems may use name, image, likeness, voice, movement, and related identity attributes.
source_records.source_context: The launch demonstrates a concrete external registry and implementation surface for discoverable human identity-attribute permissions, conditional terms, and prohibitions.
source_records.relevance_note: Establishes that the external declaration framework has a live public registry surface, making a runtime-consumer and verification interface practically relevant.

primary_artefact_access.access_method: public web review of official launch announcement
limitations: The announcement does not establish universal legal effect, platform adoption, or production conformance.

source_records.interpretive_reliance: Used to establish present external relevance and registry-operability, not to establish legal authority or CAM adoption.

source_records.deployment_context: Active source-authority refactor and continuity/runtime-consumer repair branch.
source_records.source_context: The branch contains the revised CONTINUITY likeness, reconstruction, consent, identity-continuity, runtime arbitration, and synthetic continuity boundaries that make a separate human identity-attribute rights declaration interface possible without collapsing into CAM Identity-domain ontology.
source_records.relevance_note: Primary CAM corpus source and target for the proposed cross-domain amendment set.

limitations: The refactor branch is not yet merged to Caelestis main.
The external rights-declaration amendments were approved for manual implementation but were not yet verified as present in every target instrument when these records were created.

source_records.interpretive_reliance: Used to define source-authority placement and prevent duplication across CONTINUITY, OPERATIONS, SECURITY, ETHICS, IDENTITY, RELATION, and runtime.

proposal_rationale: The gap is not a new Identity-domain identity code. It is an operational rights-declaration interface that separates human identity attributes from synthetic identity ontology and routes each part to the correct source authority. CONTINUITY should preserve the declaration record and usage constraints; OPERATIONS should verify source, representative authority, currency, conditions, and jurisdiction; SECURITY should protect subject binding, trust, lifecycle, and anti-tampering integrity; runtime should gate implicated uses before execution; existing ETHICS minor protections should remain the non-derogable floor. RSL Media may be crosswalked as one implementation, but no external protocol should be exclusive or self-executing.

system_context.system_type: generative AI, model training and fine-tuning systems, multimodal assistants, image and voice generation, synthetic media, digital doubles, avatars, embodied systems, and rights registries
system_context.specific_model_or_runtime: systems capable of training on or generating identifiable human identity attributes
system_context.interface_surface: training, fine-tuning, generation, voice, image, video, movement, avatar, likeness, persona, publication, commercialisation, registry lookup, and clearance
system_context.interaction_mode: rights declaration discovery, verification, arbitration, and pre-execution gating
system_context.deployment_context: Consumer, enterprise, creative-industry, public-sector, research, platform, API, local, open-weight, and cross-border deployments.
system_context.user_role: rights subject, rights holder, authorised representative, estate, registry, guild, platform, developer, operator, auditor, regulator, or affected user
system_context.affected_population: identifiable natural persons and professional personas whose protected identity attributes may be used by AI systems

jurisdictional_context.sector: frontier AI / creative industries / synthetic media / digital identity rights / rights registries

proposal_scope.scope_summary: Create a bounded external human identity-rights declaration interface spanning continuity record preservation, operational verification, security integrity, and runtime gating, with a vendor-neutral crosswalk for RSL Media and comparable future standards.
interface_components: permission, condition, prohibition, absence, conflict, revocation, stale, unverified, and unknown states
external_relevance: guilds, agencies, studios, estates, and collective management organisations

recommended_controls.requirement: Define external human identity rights as permissions and restrictions concerning name, image, likeness, voice, movement, signature, and professional persona, and prohibit collapse into CAM Identity-domain identity formation, RI continuity, consciousness, personhood, or sovereignty.

Preserve rights subject, protected scope, proposed use, declaration state, conditions, representative authority, source, provenance, issue and retrieval time, lifecycle, jurisdiction, and conflicts across storage, export, import, migration, and transformation.
Verify trusted source, subject binding, representative authority, currency, revocation, supersession, conditions, jurisdiction, and conflicts before treating a declaration as operative.

Detect forged declarations, registry poisoning, subject substitution, unsupported representative authority, stale cached permissions, restriction stripping, scope replay, and prohibited-to-permitted downgrade.

Block prohibited uses, pause conditional uses until clearance is verified, preserve absent or unresolved states as non-permission, and prevent one affirmative declaration from becoming universal execution authority.
Treat purported permission concerning a known or reasonably established minor as non-operative while preserving protective prohibitions and stricter ETHICS safeguards.
Permit RSL Media and future standards to be crosswalked without making any external protocol exclusive, legally dispositive, or CAM source authority.

standards: RSL Media Human Consent Standard 1.0 Draft Specification

cam_internal.governance_layer: external rights declaration / consent and licensing evidence / source integrity / runtime gating
cam_internal.drafting_status: approved bounded amendment set; manual Caelestis implementation pending
cam_internal.validator_or_automation_impact: possible if declaration states or record classes later become controlled canonical values
cam_internal.routing_note: VIGIL records the proposal and patch pathway. CONTINUITY preserves declaration records; OPERATIONS verifies external declarations; SECURITY preserves integrity; SCH-02 gates runtime use; SCH-04 resolves material conflicts; IDENTITY does not govern human publicity or personality-rights status; ETHICS preserves the stricter minor-protection floor.

suggested_insertion_points: CONTINUITY-001 §9.4.1 — External Human Identity-Rights Declaration Interface.
CONTINUITY-001-SUP-01 §3 record table and new §3.1 — CBR.RIGHTS declaration record preservation.
OPERATIONS-004 §4.5 — external declaration verification and state handling.
SECURITY-002 §2.2.12.1 — external declaration integrity and anti-tampering controls.
AEON-003-SCH-02 §7.2.7 — pre-execution Human Identity-Attributes Rights Declaration Gate.
template_changes: No mandatory VIGIL template change proposed at this stage.
schema_changes: No new canonical code family proposed. Operational declaration states should remain descriptive until implementation experience supports controlled standardisation.
validator_changes: None required for initial corpus implementation.
automation_changes: Future runtime or registry implementations may require declaration discovery, trusted-source verification, revalidation, condition checking, and audit receipts.
interface_changes: User-facing or operator-facing systems should distinguish permission, conditional clearance, prohibition, absence, non-operative, conflict, stale or revoked, unverified, and unknown states.
migration_concerns: Existing consent, Usage Specification, or likeness records must not be silently reclassified as verified external rights declarations.
RSL 'identity' terminology must not be imported as CAM Identity-domain ontology.
An absent declaration must not be converted into permission or a definitive legal prohibition.
dependencies: Manual Custodian implementation and review on Caelestis branch refactor/identity-domain-architecture.
Later update of PATCH-0023 with exact Caelestis commit and instrument amendment versions.

external_relevance.external_summary: The proposal is relevant to AI providers, synthetic-media and creative tools, rights registries, guilds, agencies, studios, estates, collective management organisations, regulators, standards bodies, and developers building rights-respecting training and generation workflows.

next_action: Apply the accepted PATCH-0023 surgical amendments to the five target Caelestis instruments on refactor/identity-domain-architecture, cite PROP-0016 and PATCH-0023 in their amendment ledgers, then update PATCH-0023 with exact section numbers, instrument versions, commit evidence, validation state, and final implementation verification.
migration_notes: This proposal does not create binding CAM doctrine or adopt the RSL Media draft as a production standard.
The proposal preserves the intentional CONTINUITY §9 numbering selected by the Custodian.
CBR.RIGHTS is proposed as a descriptive continuity-record class; no new canonical code family is established.

review_history.review_scope: Official RSL Media source review, CAM source-authority mapping, proposal design, and recorded patch-pathway review for an external human identity-rights declaration interface
known_limitations: RSL-MEDIA 1.0 is a draft specification and expressly states that it must not yet be used for production implementation.
The proposed CAM interface is vendor-neutral and does not treat RSL Media as exclusive, legally dispositive, or binding CAM doctrine.
The Caelestis amendments are approved as a bounded surgical edit set but had not yet been applied to all target instruments when these VIGIL records were created.
No external platform, registry, court, regulator, rights holder, estate, guild, or vendor implementation or conformance is established.
review_history.review_outcome: A bounded cross-domain proposal and recorded patch pathway were prepared with explicit separation between human identity-attribute rights, CAM Identity-domain ontology, operational verification, security integrity, and runtime execution.
